task_context.description: Design and simulate a homogeneous electromagnetic calorimeter using Monte Carlo methods to evaluate its performance for precision electron energy measurements. This involves creating a detector geometry using single dense scintillating crystals (no absorber materials), running Geant4 simulations with electron beams at multiple energies, and analyzing the detector response to determine energy resolution, linearity, and shower containment characteristics. The task requires comparing different crystal materials to identify the optimal choice for electron detection and characterizing the fundamental performance limits of homogeneous calorimeter designs.
task_context.goal: Design an optimal homogeneous electromagnetic calorimeter for precision measurement of electrons
constraints: Must be homogeneous calorimeter (single dense scintillating crystal)
Cannot use shashlik or accordion geometries (sampling calorimeters)
Must use tractable simulation parameters for GPU parallel processing
Particles must be shot at detector center
Must use electrons (e-) in Geant4 particle gun
